answer_key.q28: G. Functions of understanding communication styles
answer_key.q30: D. A lively and positive attitude person depicted
answer_key.q31: I. Calm and Factual personality illustrated

passages.title: Communicating Conflict
passages.source_html:
Communicating Conflict 


A 


As far back as Hippocrates’s time (460-370 B.C.), people have tried to understand other people by characterizing them according to personality type or temperament. Hippocrates believed there were four different body fluids that influenced four basic types of temperament. His work was further developed 500 years later by Galen. These days there is any number of self-assessment tools that relate to the basic descriptions developed by Galen, although we no longer believe the source to be the types of body fluid that dominate our systems.


B 


The values in self-assessments that help determine personality style. Learning styles, communication styles, conflict-handling styles, or other aspects of individuals is that they help depersonalize conflict in interpersonal relationships. The depersonalization occurs when you realize that others aren’t trying to be difficult, but they need different or more information than you do. They’re not intending to be rude: they are so focused on the task they forget about greeting people. They would like to work faster but not at the risk of damaging the relationships needed to get the job done. They understand there is a job to do. But it can only be done right with the appropriate information, which takes time to collect. When used appropriately, understanding communication styles can help resolve conflict on teams. Very rarely are conflicts true personality issues. Usually, they are issues of style, information needs, or focus.


C


Hippocrates and later Galen determined there were four basic temperaments: sanguine, phlegmatic, melancholic and choleric. These descriptions were developed centuries ago and are still somewhat apt, although you could update the wording. In today’s world, they translate into the four fairly common communication styles described below:


D 


The sanguine person would be the expressive or spirited style of communication. These people speak in pictures. They invest a lot of emotion and energy in their communication and often speak quickly. Putting their whole body into it. They are easily sidetracked onto a story that may or may not illustrate the point they are trying to make. Because of their enthusiasm, they are great team motivators. They are concerned about people and relationships. Their high levels of energy can come on strong at times and their focus is usually on the bigger picture, which means they sometimes miss the details or the proper order of things. These people find conflict or differences of opinion invigorating and love to engage in a spirited discussion. They love change and are constantly looking for new and exciting adventures.


E  


Tile phlegmatic person – cool and persevering – translates into the technical or systematic communication style. This style of communication is focused on facts and technical details. Phlegmatic people have an orderly methodical way of approaching tasks, and their focus is very much on the task, not on the people, emotions, or concerns that the task may evoke. The focus is also more on the details necessary to accomplish a task. Sometimes the details overwhelm the big picture and focus needs to be brought back to the context of the task. People with this style think the facts should speak for themselves, and they are not as comfortable with conflict. They need time to adapt to change and need to understand both the logic of it and the steps involved.


F 


Tile melancholic person who is softhearted and oriented toward doing things for others translates into the considerate or sympathetic communication style. A person with this communication style is focused on people and relationships. They are good listeners and do things for other people – sometimes to the detriment of getting things done for themselves. They want to solicit everyone’s opinion and make sure everyone is comfortable with whatever is required to get the job done. At times this focus on others can distract from the task at hand. Because they are so concerned with the needs of others and smoothing over issues, they do not like conflict. They believe that change threatens the status quo and tends to make people feel uneasy, so people with this communication style, like phlegmatic people, need time to consider the changes in order to adapt to them.


G 


The choleric temperament translates into the bold or direct style of communication. People with this style are brief in their communication – the fewer words the better. They are big-picture thinkers and love to be involved in many things at once. They are focused on tasks and outcomes and often forget that the people involved in carrying out the tasks have needs. They don’t do detail work easily and as a result, can often underestimate how much time it takes to achieve the task. Because they are so direct, they often seem forceful and can be very intimidating to others. They usually would welcome someone challenging them. But most other styles are afraid to do so. They also thrive on change, the more the better.


H 


A well-functioning team should have all of these communications styles for true effectiveness. All teams need to focus on the task, and they need to take care of relationships in order to achieve those tasks. They need the big picture perspective or the context of their work, and they need the details to be identified and taken care of for success. We all have aspects of each style within us. Some of us can easily move from one style to another and adapt our style to the needs of the situation at hand-whether the focus is on tasks or relationships. For others, a dominant style is very evident, and it is more challenging to see the situation from the perspective of another style. The work environment can influence communication styles either by the type of work that is required or by the predominance of one style reflected in that environment. Some people use one style at work and another at home. The good news about communication styles is that we have the ability to develop flexibility in our styles. The greater the flexibility we have, the more skilled we usually are at handling possible and actual conflicts. Usually, it has to be relevant to us to do so, either because we think it is important or because there are incentives in our environment to encourage it. The key is that we have to want to become flexible with our communication style. As Henry Ford said, ‘Whether you think you can or you can’t, you’re right!’

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-34


The passage has eight sections A-H. Choose the correct heading for each section from the list of headings below. 

Options (Word Bank):
A. Different personality types mentioned
B. Recommendation of combined styles for group
C. Historical explanation of understanding personality
D. A lively and positive attitude person depicted
E. A personality likes a challenge and direct communication
F. Different characters illustrated
G. Functions of understanding communication styles
H. Cautious and considerable person cited
I. Calm and Factual personality illustrated
J. Self-assessment determines one’s temperament
27
正确答案： 27 C. Historical explanation of understanding personality  |  28 G. Functions of understanding communication styles  |  29 A. Different personality types mentioned  |  30 D. A lively and positive attitude person depicted  |  31 I. Calm and Factual personality illustrated  |  32 H. Cautious and considerable person cited  |  33 E. A personality likes a challenge and direct communication  |  34 B. Recommendation of combined styles for group
Question 27

原文答案句：As far back as Hippocrates’s time (460-370 B.C.), people have tried to understand other people by characterizing them according to personality type or temperament. Hippocrates believed there were four different body fluids that influenced four basic types of temperament. His work was further developed 500 years later by Galen. 

对应段落：A段

审题：题目要求为A段选择一个合适的标题，概括该段的主要内容。

定位关键信息：通过关键词"Hippocrates"、"Galen"、"historical"、"understanding personality"等定位到A段，该段主要讲述了从古希腊时期至今，人们试图通过性格类型或气质来理解他人，并提到了希波克拉底和盖伦对气质理论的历史贡献。

解题思路：A段主要讲述了历史上对理解性格类型的研究，特别是希波克拉底和盖伦的工作。选项C"Historical explanation of understanding personality"（历史上对性格的理解）准确概括了该段内容，即从古希腊时期开始，人们如何通过性格类型或气质来理解他人，并提到了希波克拉底和盖伦对气质理论的历史贡献。因此，选择C作为A段的标题。


Question 28

原文答案句：The values in self-assessments that help determine personality style. Learning styles, communication styles, conflict-handling styles, or other aspects of individuals is that they help depersonalize conflict in interpersonal relationships.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目要求为B段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：阅读B段内容，重点关注段落的首句和尾句，以及段落中反复出现的核心词汇。

解题思路：B段主要讲述了自我评估工具在确定个性风格、学习风格、沟通风格和冲突处理风格等方面的价值，特别是它们有助于在人际关系中使冲突去个人化。段落中多次提到"self-assessments"、"personality style"、"communication styles"和"conflict"，强调了这些工具在理解和处理人际冲突中的作用。因此，选择标题G"Functions of understanding communication styles"（理解沟通风格的意义）能够准确概括B段的核心内容。


Question 29

原文答案句：Hippocrates and later Galen determined there were four basic temperaments: sanguine, phlegmatic, melancholic and choleric. These descriptions were developed centuries ago and are still somewhat apt, although you could update the wording.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目要求为C段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过段落首句的关键词"Hippocrates and later Galen"以及"four basic temperaments"可以定位到C段的核心内容是关于四种基本气质类型的介绍。

解题思路：C段主要介绍了希波克拉底和盖伦确定的四种基本气质类型（sanguine, phlegmatic, melancholic and choleric），并说明这些描述虽然古老，但仍有一定适用性。因此，该段落的标题应与"四种不同的气质类型"相关。选项A（Four basic temperaments）准确概括了段落内容，符合题意。


Question 30

原文答案句：The sanguine person would be the expressive or spirited style of communication. 

对应段落：D段

审题：题目要求为D段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过段落首句"The sanguine person would be the expressive or spirited style of communication"可以定位到D段的主要内容是关于 sanguine 这种性格类型对应的沟通风格。

解题思路：D段详细描述了 乐观型这种性格类型的特点，如善于表达、充满热情、关注人际关系、喜欢变化等。这些描述与选项D"Expressive or spirited communication style"（善于表达或活泼的沟通风格）完全吻合，因此答案为D。


Question 31

原文答案句：This style of communication is focused on facts and technical details. Phlegmatic people have an orderly methodical way of approaching tasks, and their focus is very much on the task, not on the people, emotions, or concerns that the task may evoke.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求为E段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"E"定位到E段，通过阅读E段内容找到关键信息，如"focused on facts and technical details"和"orderly methodical way of approaching tasks"。

解题思路：E段主要描述了冷静型的沟通风格，强调他们注重事实和技术细节，以及有条不紊地处理任务的方式。这与选项I"Calm and Factual personality illustrated（描述冷静客观的性格）吻合，因此选择I作为E段的标题。


Question 32

原文答案句：The melancholic person who is softhearted and oriented toward doing things for others translates into the considerate or sympathetic communication style.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目要求为F段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：根据段落首句"The melancholic person who is softhearted and oriented toward doing things for others translates into the considerate or sympathetic communication style."可以定位到F段的核心内容是关于忧郁型（melancholic）性格对应的考虑周到或富有同情心的沟通风格。

解题思路：F段主要描述了忧郁型性格的特点，他们心软且倾向于为他人做事，这种性格转化为考虑周到或富有同情心的沟通风格。段落详细描述了这种沟通风格的特点，如关注人际关系、善于倾听、重视他人意见等。因此，F段的标题应与“考虑周到或富有同情心的沟通风格”相关，对应选项H。


Question 33

原文答案句：The choleric temperament translates into the bold or direct style of communication. People with this style are brief in their communication – the fewer words the better...They usually would welcome someone challenging them. But most other styles are afraid to do so. They also thrive on change, the more the better.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目要求为G段选择一个正确的标题。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"G段"定位到文章的G段，阅读该段落内容。

解题思路：G段主要描述了暴躁型性格对应的沟通风格，即大胆或直接的沟通方式。段落详细描述了这种沟通风格的特点，如简洁、关注大局、任务导向、直接且欢迎挑战等。因此，G段的主题是关于暴躁型性格的沟通风格，选择E项。


Question 34

原文答案句：A well-functioning team should have all of these communications styles for true effectiveness. All teams need to focus on the task, and they need to take care of relationships in order to achieve those tasks. They need the big picture perspective or the context of their work, and they need the details to be identified and taken care of for success. 

对应段落：H段

审题：题目要求为H段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：根据H段的内容，重点在于讨论一个高效团队需要具备所有沟通风格，以及团队成员如何适应不同的沟通风格。

解题思路：H段主要讲述了高效团队需要包含所有沟通风格，以实现任务和关系两方面的平衡。同时提到团队成员可以根据情况调整自己的沟通风格，或者有些人更倾向于一种主导风格。因此，H段的核心内容是关于团队中沟通风格的多样性和适应性，对应的标题应为B（团队最好由多种风格的人组成）。


Questions 35-39


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? In boxes 35-39 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement is true, FALSE if the statement is false, NOT GIVEN if the information is not given in the passage.


35

It is believed that sanguine people do not like variety.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：These people find conflict or differences of opinion invigorating and love to engage in a spirited discussion. They love change and are constantly looking for new and exciting adventures.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是“人们认为乐观的人不喜欢变化”这一说法是否正确。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"sanguine people"和"do not like variety"可以定位到D段，该段描述了乐观型（sanguine）人的特点。

解题思路：原文中明确提到乐观型的人“love change”（喜欢变化）并且“are constantly looking for new and exciting adventures”（不断寻找新的和令人兴奋的冒险），这与题目中“do not like variety”（不喜欢变化）的说法完全相反。因此，题目中的说法与原文信息不符，答案为False。


36

Melancholic and phlegmatic people have similar characteristics.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：They believe that change threatens the status quo and tends to make people feel uneasy, so people with this communication style, like phlegmatic people, need time to consider the changes in order to adapt to them.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“忧郁型和冷静型的人有相似的特征”是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“Melancholic and phlegmatic”定位到F段，该段描述了忧郁型（melancholic）的特征，并提到“like phlegmatic people”，说明两者有相似之处。

解题思路：F段描述了忧郁型的人关注人际关系、不喜欢冲突、对变化持谨慎态度，并明确提到“like phlegmatic people”（与冷静型的人类似），两者都需要时间来处理和适应变化，说明两者在不喜欢冲突和对变化持谨慎态度方面有相似特征。因此，题干的说法与文章信息一致，答案为True。


37

Managers often select their best employees according to personality types.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：A well-functioning team should have all of these communications styles for true effectiveness. All teams need to focus on the task, and they need to take care of relationships in order to achieve those tasks. They need the big picture perspective or the context of their work, and they need the details to be identified and taken care of for success. 

对应段落：H段

审题：题目判断“管理者经常根据性格类型来选择最佳员工”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。”

定位关键信息：通过题目的关键词“sanguine personality”和“needed most”定位到H段。

解题思路：原文明确指出一个高效团队需要所有沟通风格（包括乐观型、冷静型、忧郁型和暴躁型），并未说明某种类型的人是最佳的。也并未提及管理者选择员工的标准，因此信息未给出。答案为Not Given。


38

It is possible for someone to change a type of personality.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：  Some of us can easily move from one style to another and adapt our style to the needs of the situation at hand-whether the focus is on tasks or relationships. For others, a dominant style is very evident, and it is more challenging to see the situation from the perspective of another style.  

对应段落：H段  

审题：题目问“一个人可以改变性格类型”是否正确。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“change a type of personality”定位到文章中关于性格或沟通风格是否可以改变的内容。  

解题思路：H段提到“Some of us can easily move from one style to another and adapt our style to the needs of the situation at hand”（有些人可以轻松地从一种风格转换到另一种风格，并根据需要适应情况），说明人的沟通风格（与性格相关）是可以调整和改变的。因此，题目中的说法与原文一致，答案为TRUE。


39

Work surrounding can affect which communication style is the most effective.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：The work environment can influence communication styles either by the type of work that is required or by the predominance of one style reflected in that environment.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问工作环境是否会影响最佳沟通风格。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"Work surrounding"（工作环境）和"communication style"（沟通风格）定位到H段。

解题思路：原文明确提到"工作环境可以影响沟通风格，要么是通过不同的工作类型，要么是通过该环境所体现的主流沟通风格"。这说明工作环境确实会影响哪种沟通风格最有效，因此题目表述与原文信息一致，答案为TRUE。


Question 40


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D. Write your answers in boxes 40 on your answer sheet.


40

The writer believes using self-assessment tools can

正确答案： B. help to understand colleagues and resolve problems.

原文答案句：The values in self-assessments that help determine personality style. Learning styles, communication styles, conflict-handling styles, or other aspects of individuals is that they help depersonalize conflict in interpersonal relationships.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问作者认为自我评估工具能够做到什么。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"self-assessment tools"，在文章中定位到B段，该段提到了自我评估工具的作用。

解题思路：根据原文答案句可知，自我评估工具可以帮助理解人际关系中的冲突，并帮助解决这些问题。因此，选项B"help to understand colleagues and resolve problems"（帮助理解同事，解决工作中的问题与原文意思一致，是正确答案。其他选项A、C、D都与原文不符。
